documentedChange.value: Mondelez launched a '30% Less Sugar' variant (about 37g sugar per 100g, against about 56g for the standard bar) using soluble maize fibre; it was delisted by October 2023. The standard bar's recipe was not reduced.
documentedChange.note: The 2019 launch reports are likely co-derived from a single Mondelez press release, so their lineage independence is weak; this is treated as authoritative-attribution rather than independently corroborated. No archival primary label was obtained.

statedReason.value: We've recognised that there is an increasing trend for people wanting to manage their sugar intake and that's why we have worked tirelessly to create a Cadbury Dairy Milk bar with 30% less sugar, which still tastes great. (Claire Low, Associate Marketing Director, Cadbury, July 2019)

labelledInference.basis: The change created a separate lower-sugar product rather than reducing the flagship recipe, and Mondelez did not name the FSA or PHE sugar-reduction programme.
labelledInference.text: The lower-sugar variant is consistent with the incentive of the FSA sugar-reduction programme, but Mondelez framed it as consumer-demand-led and did not attribute it to the programme.
